note: AIVORA adaptive diagnostic. kind: gs (knowledge) | csat (comprehension/reasoning). difficulty: 1 easy, 2 medium, 3 hard. Server-authoritative answer keys; never sent to the client.

items.subject: Polity
items.text: Fundamental Rights are enshrined in which Part of the Indian Constitution?
items.explanation: Fundamental Rights are in Part III (Articles 12–35).

items.subject: Polity
items.text: Who is the constitutional head of the Indian Union?
items.explanation: The President of India is the constitutional head of the Union executive (Article 52–53).

items.subject: Polity
items.text: The Directive Principles of State Policy are:

options.D: Applicable only to the Union government

items.explanation: DPSPs (Part IV) are non-justiciable but fundamental in the governance of the country (Article 37).

items.subject: Polity
items.text: Which Article empowers the Supreme Court to issue writs for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights?
items.explanation: Article 32 is itself a Fundamental Right; Article 226 gives similar (wider) writ power to High Courts.

items.subject: Polity
items.text: The doctrine of 'basic structure' of the Constitution was propounded in which case?
items.explanation: The basic structure doctrine was laid down in Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala (1973).

items.subject: History
items.text: The Indian National Congress was founded in which year?
items.explanation: The INC was founded in 1885, with A.O. Hume playing a key role.

items.subject: History
items.text: The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi in:
items.explanation: The Non-Cooperation Movement began in 1920, following the Jallianwala Bagh massacre and Khilafat issue.

items.subject: History
items.text: Which session of the Indian National Congress first adopted the resolution of 'Purna Swaraj' (complete independence)?
items.explanation: The Lahore session (1929), presided over by Jawaharlal Nehru, adopted Purna Swaraj; 26 Jan 1930 was observed as Independence Day.

items.subject: Geography
items.text: The Tropic of Cancer does NOT pass through which of these Indian states?
items.explanation: The Tropic of Cancer passes through 8 states but not Tamil Nadu, which lies entirely to its south.

items.subject: Geography
items.text: Which one of the following rivers is a west-flowing river of peninsular India?
items.explanation: The Narmada (and Tapi) flow westward into the Arabian Sea through rift valleys; the others flow east into the Bay of Bengal.

items.subject: Geography
items.text: The phenomenon of 'El Niño' is associated with:

options.B: Warming of the central and eastern Pacific Ocean
options.C: Warming of the Indian Ocean only

items.explanation: El Niño is the abnormal warming of central-eastern Pacific surface waters, often weakening the Indian monsoon.

items.subject: Economy
items.text: Which institution in India is responsible for monetary policy?
items.explanation: The RBI formulates and implements monetary policy in India.

items.subject: Economy
items.text: 'Fiscal deficit' is best defined as:

options.A: Total expenditure minus total receipts excluding borrowings

items.explanation: Fiscal deficit = total expenditure − total receipts (excluding borrowings); it indicates the borrowing requirement of the government.

items.subject: Economy
items.text: An increase in the Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR) by the RBI will generally:

options.A: Increase the lending capacity of banks
options.B: Reduce liquidity in the banking system
options.C: Have no effect on money supply

items.explanation: A higher CRR locks up more of banks' deposits with the RBI, reducing lendable funds and liquidity.

items.subject: Environment
items.text: Which gas is the most significant contributor to the greenhouse effect from human activity?
items.explanation: CO2 from fossil-fuel burning is the largest anthropogenic contributor to the greenhouse effect.

items.subject: Environment
items.text: The 'Ramsar Convention' is associated with the conservation of:
items.explanation: The Ramsar Convention (1971) is an international treaty for the conservation and wise use of wetlands.

items.subject: Science & Technology
items.text: India's Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) was launched by which organisation?
items.explanation: ISRO launched the Mars Orbiter Mission in 2013; India became the first nation to reach Mars orbit on its first attempt.

items.subject: Logical Reasoning
items.text: Find the next number in the series: 2, 4, 8, 16, ?
items.explanation: Each term doubles the previous one: 16 × 2 = 32.

items.subject: Numeracy
items.text: If a shirt costs ₹800 after a 20% discount, what was its original price?
items.explanation: 800 is 80% of the original; original = 800 / 0.8 = ₹1000.

items.subject: Logical Reasoning
items.text: All roses are flowers. Some flowers fade quickly. Which conclusion necessarily follows?

options.C: No definite conclusion about roses fading can be drawn

items.explanation: 'Some flowers fade quickly' need not include roses; no necessary conclusion about roses follows.

items.subject: Numeracy
items.text: A train travels 360 km in 4 hours. Maintaining the same speed, how far will it travel in 7 hours?
items.explanation: Speed = 360/4 = 90 km/h; in 7 hours = 90 × 7 = 630 km.

items.subject: Data Interpretation
items.text: A student scored 45, 60, 75 and 80 in four tests. What is the average score?

items.subject: Logical Reasoning
items.text: In a code, MONDAY is written as NPOEBZ. How is FRIDAY written in the same code?
items.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by one: F→G, R→S, I→J, D→E, A→B, Y→Z, giving GSJEBZ.

items.subject: Numeracy
items.text: The ratio of two numbers is 3:5 and their sum is 64. What is the larger number?
items.explanation: Parts = 3+5 = 8; one part = 64/8 = 8; larger = 5 × 8 = 40.

items.subject: Reading Comprehension
items.passage: Public trust in institutions is not built through grand declarations but through the quiet, consistent reliability of everyday action. When a citizen finds that a service works as promised, time after time, confidence accumulates almost invisibly. It is the breach — the one unkept promise — that is remembered, while a thousand kept ones pass unnoticed.
items.text: According to the passage, public trust is built primarily by:
items.explanation: The passage states trust is built through 'quiet, consistent reliability of everyday action'.

items.subject: Reading Comprehension
items.passage: Public trust in institutions is not built through grand declarations but through the quiet, consistent reliability of everyday action. When a citizen finds that a service works as promised, time after time, confidence accumulates almost invisibly. It is the breach — the one unkept promise — that is remembered, while a thousand kept ones pass unnoticed.
items.text: Which statement best captures the author's implied view?

options.A: Institutions should publicise their successes loudly
options.B: A single failure can disproportionately damage accumulated trust
options.C: Citizens rarely notice whether services work
options.D: Grand declarations are the fastest route to trust

items.explanation: The passage implies the remembered 'breach' outweighs many unnoticed successes — a single failure disproportionately harms trust.

items.subject: Polity
items.text: The Preamble declares India to be a:
items.explanation: The Preamble (as amended by the 42nd Amendment) declares India a Sovereign Socialist Secular Democratic Republic.

items.subject: Polity
items.text: Which statement about the Rajya Sabha is correct?

options.A: It can be dissolved like the Lok Sabha
options.B: It is a permanent house; one-third of members retire every two years
options.C: Its members are directly elected by the people
options.D: It has a fixed term of five years

items.explanation: The Rajya Sabha is a permanent house that is never fully dissolved; one-third of its members retire every second year.

items.subject: History
items.text: Who is known as the 'Iron Man of India'?
items.explanation: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, who integrated the princely states, is called the Iron Man of India.

items.subject: History
items.text: The Salt Satyagraha (Dandi March) was launched by Gandhi in the year:
items.explanation: The Dandi March / Salt Satyagraha was launched in March 1930 as part of the Civil Disobedience Movement.

items.subject: Geography
items.text: The southwest monsoon brings most of India's annual rainfall during which months?
items.explanation: The southwest monsoon (June–September) accounts for the bulk of India's annual rainfall.

items.subject: Economy
items.text: GDP stands for:
items.explanation: GDP = Gross Domestic Product, the total value of goods and services produced within a country.

items.subject: Economy
items.text: The Goods and Services Tax (GST) in India is a:
items.explanation: GST is an indirect tax levied on the supply of goods and services.

items.subject: Environment
items.text: The Montreal Protocol is associated with the phasing out of:
items.explanation: The Montreal Protocol (1987) phases out ozone-depleting substances such as CFCs.

items.subject: Science & Technology
items.text: India's first satellite, launched in 1975, was named:
items.explanation: Aryabhata, India's first satellite, was launched in 1975.

items.subject: Logical Reasoning
items.text: Find the odd one out: 3, 5, 7, 9, 11
items.explanation: 3, 5, 7 and 11 are prime; 9 (3×3) is the only composite number.

items.subject: Numeracy
items.text: What is 15% of 200?

items.subject: Logical Reasoning
items.text: A is the brother of B. B is the sister of C. How is A related to C?
items.explanation: A is male (brother) and is a sibling of C, so A is the brother of C.

items.subject: Numeracy
items.text: A sum of money doubles in 5 years under simple interest. What is the annual rate of interest?
items.explanation: Doubling means interest equals the principal: P = P×r×5/100 → r = 20%.

items.subject: Data Interpretation
items.text: The average of 5 numbers is 30. When one number is removed, the average of the remaining four becomes 28. What number was removed?
items.explanation: Sum of 5 = 150; sum of 4 = 112; removed = 150 − 112 = 38.

items.subject: Reading Comprehension
items.passage: A skill is rarely mastered in a single burst of effort. It is built through repeated, deliberate practice — each small, focused session adding a little to what came before, until competence feels almost effortless.
items.text: According to the passage, a skill is mastered mainly through:

options.C: A single intense day of effort

items.explanation: The passage states skill is built through 'repeated, deliberate practice'.

items.subject: Geography
items.text: Which is the longest river in India?
items.explanation: The Ganga (~2,525 km) is the longest river in India.

items.subject: History
items.text: The Arya Samaj was founded by:
items.explanation: Swami Dayananda Saraswati founded the Arya Samaj in 1875.

items.subject: Polity
items.text: The anti-defection provisions are contained in which Schedule of the Constitution?
items.explanation: The Tenth Schedule, added by the 52nd Amendment (1985), contains the anti-defection provisions.

items.subject: Economy
items.text: The repo rate is the rate at which the RBI:

options.B: lends short-term funds to commercial banks against securities

items.explanation: Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends short-term funds to commercial banks against government securities.

items.subject: Numeracy
items.text: If 5 pens cost ₹75, what is the cost of 8 pens at the same rate?

items.subject: Logical Reasoning
items.text: Pointing to a photograph, a man said, 'She is the daughter of my grandfather's only son.' How is the woman related to the man?
items.explanation: Grandfather's only son is the man's father; the father's daughter is the man's sister.

items.subject: Logical Reasoning
items.text: Find the next number in the series: 1, 4, 9, 16, ?
items.explanation: The series is perfect squares (1², 2², 3², 4²); next is 5² = 25.

items.subject: Reading Comprehension
items.passage: Reform that lasts is rarely dramatic. It advances quietly, adjusting course as evidence accumulates, willing to be slow if slowness is the price of getting it right.
items.text: The passage most values reform that is:
items.explanation: The passage praises reform that advances quietly and adjusts to evidence — gradual and evidence-led.
